title: sambucus racemosa f. serratifolia
intro: Sambucus racemosa f. serratifolia, also known as the mountain elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Adoxaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its berries, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to their high content of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa f. serratifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, coughs, and sore throats. In herbal preparations, the plant's berries are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
